subject: Re: No movies found
body:
I've figured out the error by now. 
My SubFramePath line had a different file name, as the frame files. I only 
had to change to the correct file name and this made it work. 

On Thursday, June 1, 2023 at 6:44:59 PM UTC+2 Roan Groh wrote:

> Dear all,
>
> I have a the same error message, but couldn't solve the problem with your 
> tips.
>
> I have tilt series, which I only have as .mrc stacks. With relion i 
> changed those to .tif files also leading to having single files per tilt 
> and not in a stack anymore. I processed these with warp and get the same 
> error message (No movies found for Auto2RiboTS01.mrc, or none have been 
> processed in Warp yet.) when I want to do the stack. I changed in the 
> mdoc files "ImageFile =" to .tif and my mdoc files are for each tilt series 
> and not for each single tilt. 
>
> I've been running warp before without an issue and can't figure out what 
> warp needs. I assume something with the mdoc files is off. 
>
> On Friday, October 22, 2021 at 4:03:50 AM UTC+2 slol...@stanford.edu 
> wrote:
>
>> Oh, and one more thing.  I also edited the .mdoc file for the correct 
>> file; the original 'ImageFile =' was a .st and not a .tif.  
>>
>> On Thursday, October 21, 2021 at 6:59:47 PM UTC-7 Stefan Oliver wrote:
>>
>>> Thanks for the prompt reply, Alister.  I figured it out eventually.  The 
>>> issue I had was that each tilt movie had its own .mdoc file.  Fortunately, 
>>> I also had .mdoc files for each tilt series.  Once that was used WARP was 
>>> fine.  What I'm curious about is that in the WARP Quick Start: Tilt 
>>> Series section (http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=378) it states " If 
>>> there are multiple mdoc files per tilt series (e. g. “plus” and “minus” 
>>> after bi-directional collection), you can specify several *suffixes*."  
>>> I wasn't clear how to use this function.  I tried a few combinations but 
>>> then figured out the alternative solution.
>>>
>>> Thanks for your feedback.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Stefan
>>>
>>> On Thursday, October 21, 2021 at 2:48:50 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi both,
>>>>
>>>> Sounds like a path inconsistency between your data and the movies - 
>>>> check against the tutorial data set and fix manually on a copy of the mdocs 
>>>> should solve it 🙂 
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>
>>>> On 21 Oct 2021, at 18:13, Stefan Oliver <slol...@stanford.edu> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> ﻿I have a similar issue with a current data set using .tif files and 
>>>> their associated .mdoc files.  When I worked with the tutorial 
>>>> data EMPIAR-10164 this portion of the pipeline did not have any issues.  I 
>>>> have a similar question, am I missing something obvious in the 2D 
>>>> processing that means the movies are not being generated?  Thanks in 
>>>> advance for your help.  
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Tuesday, October 12, 2021 at 5:31:02 AM UTC-7 Daniel Ziemianowicz 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> I'm following the quick start guide, and although everything appears 
>>>>> straightforward to me, I can't get past the first step.
>>>>>
>>>>> After specifying 1) the folder with movies and 2) folder with .mdoc 
>>>>> files I get the message "No movies found for TS_01.mrc, or none have been 
>>>>> processed in Warp yet." and I do not get a list with checkboxes visible on 
>>>>> the right side. 
>>>>>
>>>>> The directory I specify for 1 and 2 contain the .mdoc and the set of 
>>>>> individual tilt movies. I suspect I'm missing something obvious here 
>>>>> because I can't find any other similar questions in this group. 
>>>>>
>>>>> -- 
>>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>>>> To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/5c147404-ea37-4aa8-9a9d-e673948b3b5cn%40googlegroups.com 
>>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/5c147404-ea37-4aa8-9a9d-e673948b3b5cn%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>>> .
>>>>
>>>>
